-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Akeneo

Integration package published:

Akeneo PIM Categories integration with SAP Commerce

The Akeneo PIM Adapter connects Akeneo PIM with SAP Commerce Cloud, enabling the transfer of category data. This integration facilitates efficient communication between the two systems, ensuring data is properly structured and delivered.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MDP Group

Integration package published:

Exchange Rate from the Central Bank of Turkey Integration with SAP ERP

This integration, developed by MDP Group, provides an endpoint to ensure exchange rates are kept up to date in SAP ERP. By incorporating it into your existing structure, it can be utilized to retrieve exchange rate information for relevant currencies whenever needed.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Overall Summary of the Year 2024:

In the year 2024, we witnessed significant progress in the expansion of our technological and business ecosystems. One of the noteworthy achievements is the publication of 55 new partner packages on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. These packages encompass a variety of Integrations, APIs, and Validated Partner Use Cases, all designed to enhance business operations, accelerate growth, and foster innovation.

Thank you for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting developments in the future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section and discover center mission for more details.

entry.title: Defining Business Architecture: A Guide for Estimating Transformation Project
entry.content:
Why don't Business Processes go to the ERP Transformation Party?

Because they heard that "Change" will be there, and they're still trying to figure out their outfit from the last ERP update!

Problem Statement:

You are embarking on transforming your business by replacing/upgrading your 20-year-old Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. You are in the Explore phase and looking for a realistic scope and budget for implementation.

Your goal is to implement effective and efficient processes on a new platform. However, in practice, processes are often inadequately documented. Significant uncertainty arises without a clear understanding of the degree of changes required to redesign and implement processes. This uncertainty complicates the estimation of scope and budget. To reduce this uncertainty, a business architecture that provides a structured approach to understanding and estimating the transformation required at the process level must be created.

Why it’s essential to understand Business Processes

Every organization (business, government, not-for-profit) follows business processes to create value by completing tasks in a given sequence. Using processes, business users exchange information, money and materials to create value (by completing a given task). The success of the business is dependent on the efficiencies and effectiveness of these processes. They may complete all steps using paper, phone calls, and fax or use 100+ well-connected systems. These processes may not be documented at all, partially documented, or not updated in years.

Small businesses don’t formalize or document business processes, as an individual task processor's memory can hold ‘how things are done here.’ Their record-keeping systems are simpler, and transaction volume is low. They rely on an individual’s capability to get the job done. It doesn’t mean they don’t have to deal with complexities during transformation, but due to the smaller scale of their operation, they can ‘manage’ transitions in technology and market conditions.

However, in large organizations, processes span various business units, departments and IT applications. Data/process steps overlap between systems, often managed through swivel chairs (duplicate entries) and barely linked interfaces. Many process variances arise due to individual preferences as they slightly twist/bend/adapt the system to accommodate their way of working. Often, this results in adding extra business rules/logic to the application. Over the years, as people change jobs and processes evolve, this extra logic becomes ‘illogical/redundant/burden’ aka Technical Debt. It's very difficult to get rid of the technical debt because you don't know what skeletons you will find.

Typical reasons for not documenting business processes:

  1. There is no clear ownership for documenting end-to-end processes. Business analysts often work in silos of their expertise and do not cross departmental boundaries. System analysts often focus on 'system steps' and interfaces and ignore manual tasks.
  2. Business users go through the daily motions of process execution based on ‘how things are done here,’ and they have a hard time describing end-to-end processes.
  3. Users and analysts are so busy completing assigned tasks that they lack the time, energy, and motivation to stop and document the process.
  4. Documenting business processes seems enormous when you extrapolate 100s of processes across 10s of departments utilizing multiple systems (applications).

Defining business architecture iteratively early in the project will resolve the above issues. 

How do you define a business architecture for your transformation project?

Business architecture is defined with two separate artifacts

1. Business Capability Model (BCM) that describes the complete set of capabilities an organization requires to fulfill its mission. It’s typically structured hierarchically in three levels and describes WHAT an organization needs to deliver business value and succeed.

 

Figure 1 - SAP Reference Business Capability Model (BCM)

2. A Business Process Model (BPM) describes the business processes of an enterprise based on a standardized set of value-creating activities. It’s typically structured hierarchically in levels and describes HOW these value-creating activities will be performed

Figure 2 - SAP Reference Business Process Model (BPM)

Linkage between BCM and BPM:

  • The lower process levels will identify which set of capabilities a process needs to execute effectively and which processes a particular capability will serve.

You can use hierarchical elements of BCM and BPM for various purposes. A few advantages of building hierarchies are:

  • Group higher levels into ‘Strategic,’ ‘Core,’ and ‘Supporting,’ per the organization's strategic objectives.
  • Performing heat mapping for capability maturity, risks, investment requirements
  • Ease of scheduling workshops and assign capability/process owners and subject matter experts.
  • Estimate implementation and change management efforts at various levels of the hierarchy

Note: SAP Reference Business Architecture provides template/industry variants for BCM and BPM - SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub.

The Business Roles catalog and Business Data catalog are two other business architecture artifacts created in the explore phase. They can be used as input to create business activity flowcharts. This blog does not cover business capabilities (L3) linking to Solution capabilities.

Why do you need to create a business process hierarchy?

According to the SAP Activate methodology, the business scope is defined in the Explore phase of the project. You will need the following to estimate the transformation scope/effort.

  1. List of business processes that are generally in scope (to be refined in Fit-to-Standard [F2S] workshop)
  2.  Scope of change at a process level including a) effort to configure the system for to-be processes and b) change management efforts 

The above is achieved by capturing delta requirements during fit-to-standard workshops.

A business process hierarchy (BPH) can structure a list of business processes that are part of the project's overall scope (also called a business process catalog). This process is iterative and typically incomplete until all F2S workshops are complete.

How do you create a business process hierarchy?

You can utilize BCM or BPM to create BPH.

Option 1: Business Capability Model (BCM) based grouping:

  • Business Domain (L1), Business Area (L2), Business Capability (L3) + Business Activity (L4)
  • A functional view of the business [difficult to stitch together E2E process]
  • Assing business process (L4) to one prime business capability (L3). Typically, there is more than one capability required to perform a given process, so assigning a prime will make it easy to visualize the hierarchy
  • Easier to identify links between business capabilities and solution capabilities

Option 2: Business Process Model (BPM) based grouping:

  • Business Process (L1), Business Process Module (L2), Business Process Segment (L3) + Business Activity (L4)
  • Process-centric [difficult to tackle duplicate capabilities within an organization]
  • It is easier to accommodate process variances at higher levels in the hierarchy.
  • It is difficult to assign a single end-to-end process owner as processes span multiple functions.
  • The nested nature of BPM makes it challenging to visualize in a tabular list.

  Figure 3 - Business Process Hierarchy example for "Manage Purchase Order"

A Business Activity (L4) can be referenced in multiple higher-level processes/process variances. Business activities can be nested inside other business activities to break complex business activities with many steps into manageable activities.

Business Activity (Level 4):

  • A 'granular business process' creates value for internal or external stakeholders.
  • will have consistent naming conventions
  • further broken down into tasks (level 5) and steps (level 6)
  • will have a unique process flow that is defined using ‘BPMN’ diagram
    1. Steps to perform in a given sequence with decision points
    2. Application/s [input from Application catalog]
    3. Data utilized/analyzed/updated/produced [input from Business Data catalog]
    4. Roles/agents responsible for the activity [input from Business Role catalog]

Fit-to-standard workshops will evaluate business processes at this level. During these workshops, define 'to-be' Business Process, create flowcharts (in BPMN), and capture delta requirements (backlog). Use SAP Best Practices as a starting point (reference SAP Signavio Process Navigator). 

Figure 4 - Understanding links between BCM, BPM, Business Activity, Task and Steps

Tips:

  • Start with the SAP reference content and tailor it to your industry/organization needs.
  • Defining the first three levels before diving into listing business activities (L4)
  • Involve Business and IT user community

Caution:

  • You can start this exercise on a spreadsheet, but it’s not scalable and not great for collaboration.
  • Get the right tool to manage BCM (e.g. #SAP LeanIX), BPM and BPH (e.g. SAP Signavio)
  • Setup naming standards

You have now defined the Business Process Hierarchy and have the list of all required business activities (L4). You are ready to execute a fit-to-standard workshop to capture the backlog <blog - Conduct F2S>. You can summarize backlog at various hierarchy levels to scope the estimated project cost and change management efforts.

Conclusion:

Defining a business architecture is a crucial step in any transformation project. It provides a structured approach to understanding and improving the processes that drive your organization. By mapping out these processes, you can identify inefficiencies, streamline operations, and ultimately achieve your transformation goals. Remember, the success of your project depends on transforming business processes (they need to attend the party!). So, take the time to get it right; your efforts will pay off in the long run.

Reference:

  1. TOGAF Value Streams Guide
  2. SAP Signavio Process Explorer – Accessed Jan 20, 2025 SAP Signavio
  3. Process Mapping Basics: Guiding Your Organization Toward Effective Process Mapping - SAP Signavio - Accessed Jan 20, 2025
  4. The Top 5 Mistakes You Can Make When Naming Business Processes - Accessed Jan 20, 2025
  5. Book: Business Architecture: Collecting, Connecting, and Correcting the Dots, by Roger Burlton. Technics Publication

entry.title: SAP CPQ Integration with Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM)
entry.content:
Background

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) sell side adapter for SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates the contracting flow to the sell side process. New quotes created on SAP CPQ are automatically transitioned into a Contract Request on Sirion CLM enabling a sync between two systems for the entire sales and contracting flow.

The package contains:

1. Integration flows for:
    a) Initiation of Contract Draft Requests based on SAP CPQ quotes including line items
    b) Tracking the contract request status and enabling quick access to the Contract on Sirion CLM

2. Configuration guides for SAP CPQ, SAP Cloud Integration, Sirion CLM and Integration flow are available on the Documents tab.

The adapter can be configured by defining integration points, mapping data fields between SAP CPQ and Sirion CLM, setting up Sirion API authentication on SAP Cloud Integration.  

Note : This will be partner based Integration packages offered and supports for SAP CPQ.

Integration Package - SAP API Business Hub

SAP CPQ - CLM Integration Document

entry.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub domains take centerstage
entry.content:
Previous articles in this series 

  • What are business accelerators? 
  • SAP API Business Hub renamed to SAP Business Accelerator Hub 
  • Introducing SAP Business Accelerator Hub Domains

A brief recap

SAP Business Accelerator Hub now hosts a variety of content catering to, sometimes diametrically opposite persona like pro-code developer or a no code user. To ensure all users have a great experience on the site we introduced the concept of domains.

We liked it so much we removed all our previous navigations and made it our top level navigation. That is what this blog covers.

Moving domains to centerstage

We started with Integration and SAP Build domains, then we added Analytics and Transformation. 

We like this idea so much and believe in it that we moved this up to the top menu!

So where did the other menu items go? Under the Explore menu item on the top right of the window!

I really love this screen shot below, it shows 90% of the valuable things you are able to do with the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 But this does not answer where did 'Discover Integrations' go?

It is the landing page of the Integration domain which fits, right?

entry.title: Accelerate Process Innovation with Enterprise Automation: SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
The ability to intelligently automate business processes is top of mind for many stakeholders involved in process management across organizations. According to a recent IDC study, annual spending on Intelligent Process Automation (IPA) technologies is projected to reach $102 billion by 2028. Of this, $19.9 billion will be allocated to AI-powered automation tools, with an impressive annual growth rate of 43.4%. 

While there will be certainly no shortage in resources to spend for automation software, the real challenge remains in the details: How can organizations implement a holistic approach for process excellence, which allows them to manage processes, identify bottlenecks, deliver scalable automations and integrate their process extension within a holistic process landscape. In another blogpost by Manuel,we have already outlined in detail how SAP’s framework on Enterprise Automation delivers a solution to the problem statement mentioned above. 

And this blogpost series will offer you a more detailed guidance on the interconnectivity of each service included in Enterprise Automation which are: 

  • SAP Build Process Automation 
  • SAP Integration Suite 
  • and of course SAP Signavio. 

Let’s explore the value you can unlock for your processes by combining SAP Signavio with SAP Integration Suite in this one in this one. You can refer the blog post to understand SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite use case pattern. Also, refer the blog post to understand SAP Signavio and SAP Build use case pattern.

 

What is Enterprise Automation? 

As outlined in previous blogpost Enterprise Automation with SAP: Enterprise automation with SAP software provides an integrated set of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and optimization of your processes and workflows. It helps you quickly and intuitively gain insights into your business processes and identify the right automation tool for the right outcome. While each solution can be leveraged independently, Enterprise Automation offers native integration between SAP Build, SAP integration Suite and SAP Signavio – supplemented with best practices, guidance and pre-build content which can be implemented directly. 

 

 

 

 

  • SAP Signavio provides visibility into business processes and potential bottlenecks, root-cause analysis, and suggestions for improvement based on 200,000 industry benchmarks and best practices. This detailed understanding of inefficiencies across your organization enables you to optimize and automate processes. 

 

  • SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform as a service with thousands of prebuilt integrations, APIs, events and connectors to SAP and third-party software applications. The suite reduces complexity across on-premise and multi-cloud environments, helping you optimize comprehensive business processes. 

 

  • SAP Build to create automations and applications using drag-and-dropthe low-code and no-code design tools. Including pre-built automations and connectors for SAP software and third- party systems, the solutions let your business experts rapidly address process inefficiencies 

 

Here you can also see a visualization of the main components and its respective capabilities: 

 

 

Leverage the combined power of SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite 

 

1st scenario: Mine Third Party Data

 To identify inefficiencies and biggest automation potential in your processes, Signavio lets you consistently mine data within and across your IT software systems by leveraging the power of SAP Integration Suite.

You can leverage the broad range of connectors and adapters offered by SAP Integration Suite to seamlessly integrate with any business application by designing your own iflows or by leveraging  pre-built content.

 

In the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you’ll  find pre-built content connected to common third-party applications such as Salesforce, Coupa , Microsoft dynamics and many more to come.

Each integration flow serves the purpose of replicating business objects and their associated events from third-party systems into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

 The combined power of SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite helps you jump-start process mining initiatives and save time with ready-to-use content for multiple SAP and non SAP systems. You can now effectively prioritize your process mining efforts.

 Please refer the blogpost by Hoang on how to leverage pre-built content from non-SAP system like Salesforce.

If you would like to develop your own connectivity logic using Cloud Integration capability ,please refer the developer tutorial.

 

2nd  scenario: Connect mining to third party tools

 Another pattern to trigger processes without human intervention based on insights gained is using Cloud Integration capability.

Some of the scenarios in which the pattern could be leveraged be:

Automated Correction of Master Data:

 SAP Signavio Process Insights can identify issues such as incorrect or incomplete master data. Based on this insight, an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite could be triggered to automatically correct or enrich the master data in the relevant SAP or non SAP system

Dynamic Customer Notifications:

 If SAP Signavio Process Insights detects delays in the order-to-cash process, SAP Integration Suite could trigger notifications to customers regarding potential shipping delays or other relevant updates.

Automated Invoice Reconciliation: 

SAP Signavio Process Insights can identify discrepancies in invoice processing.This could trigger an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite to automatically reconcile invoices with corresponding purchase orders and goods receipts.

Proactive Inventory Management: 

When SAP Signavio Process Insights identifies potential stockouts or overstock situations, SAP Integration Suite could trigger an integration to adjust safety stock levels, initiate replenishment orders, or optimize warehouse operations.

Please refer documentation to understand how to configure cloud integration based actions.

 

 

3rd scenario: Event Driven Architectures

 To be able to continuously improve processes in real time, Process analyst can configure actions in SAP Signavio based on insights gained using process data.

Using Event Mesh capability of SAP Integration suite, any SAP or non-SAP processes can be triggered for improvement. Thereby, allowing the system to proactively identify issues and initiate corrective actions based on real-time monitoring of process performance. 

Examples of event-based process improvements triggered by SIGNAL queries (SAP Signavio Analytical Language) being run in SAP Signavio:

Alerting on order delays:

If a SIGNAL query detects that an order is taking significantly longer than usual to process, it could publish an event to trigger a workflow to the order management team to investigate and take corrective action in a non-SAP system

Escalating customer support issues:

When a SIGNAL query identifies a high volume of customer support tickets related to a specific product, it can automatically trigger a workflow in a non-SAP system by publishing an event to escalate the issue to a higher tier support team.

Dynamic resource allocation:

Based on real-time workload data from a SIGNAL query, an event can be published to trigger a non SAP process that could automatically reassign tasks to available team members to optimize capacity utilization. 

Please refer the help documentation as to how the event-based actions are configured by a Process Analyst in SAP Signavio.

 

Conclusion 

I hope this blog post has helped you identify the key synergies between SAP Signavio and SAP Integration Suite and how they can enable seamless automation across your enterprise. Please remember that the use cases provided are just examples and can be tailored and refined to meet your specific needs. 

For further guidance, I’ve included links to additional blog posts and resources on the Help Portal that will assist you in implementing these use cases. If you're interested in exploring more scenarios related to Enterprise Automation, feel free to check out the following links: 

 

  • Official Enterprise Automation with SAP page 
  • How to get started with Enterprise Automation Guide 
  • Whitepaper: Transition to SAP S/4HANA with Enterprise Automation 
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP Product Tour 
  • Enterprise Automation with SAP webinar

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q1 ’25)

In continuation of our previous blog, we are pleased to share further updates and maintain the momentum of delivering relevant insights to our community.

entry.title: Extract SAP Ariba data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Overview

To succeed with end-to-end digital procurement transformation, we at SAP Signavio aim to cover the whole process and IT landscape of our customers, comprising SAP, SAP Ariba as well as external systems.

I am happy to share the release of our out-of-the-box integration content for SAP Ariba connectivity to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence that you can use to perform process mining analysis on end-to-end procurement processes.

Challenge

Currently existing integration between SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Ariba is cumbersome and relies on a customized BTP app and consulting services for modifications.

 
Value proposition
Seamlessly connect to SAP Ariba using native 'Cloud Integration' from the SAP Integration Suite. This streamlined integration enables efficient data extraction from SAP Ariba into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, unlocking powerful insights and process mining capabilities for procurement workflows.
 
Benefits
  • Reuse integration capabilities and preconfigured integration packages for SAP Ariba in SAP Cloud Integration data services
  • Provide insights into procurement process inefficiencies, enabling data-driven decision-making and continuous process improvement

How to get started?

Let’s jointly explore where you can find the data extractor content and how to use it.

First, launch the SAP Business Accelerator Hub at API.SAP.COM, and navigate to SAP Signavio.

Then select the SAP Ariba integration:

Next, select the SAP Ariba API Extractor for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

A brief overview sketches the purpose of this data extractor. Once, navigated to the “Integration Flow” tab, the following view appears:

 

For each of these integration flows, more information such as configuration file templates, user manuals and change logs are available for download. For illustration, the screenshot below shows the information flow of the Data Extractor.

Install Data Extractor in SAP Integration Suite

If this Data Extractor meets your goals, log into the SAP Integration and navigate to “Discover/Integration”.

 

In this search window, search for “Ariba API”, and the new Data Extractor will appear.

Once you select the package “SAP Ariba API Extractor for Signavio Process Intelligence”, more information regarding the included objects is provided.

To install the package, just click on “Copy” to transfer the package in our design area for configuration and deployment.

If this step is completed: Congratulations, the new package is installed. Finally, connect the pipelines in Process Intelligence with the Data Extractor and enjoy analysing your SAP Ariba processes in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

 

To get you started, here some further links:

SAP Signavio on SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Content: SAP Ariba Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

If you don't want to start from scratch, have a look at the following two Value Accelerators, which already use this Data Extractor:

  • Procure to Pay in SAP Ariba
  • Source Product and Services in SAP Ariba

entry.title: Helpful links for your SAP transformation
entry.content:
The architecture chapter can use and recommend the tools and services below to guide and equip (and create principles and guardrails to) professionals and managers to support evidence-based decisions for your business and digital transformations.

The links below can help to provide visibility and clarity of your SAP ecosystem, especially to non-SAP professionals and leaders, so they can also make better questions when discovering and exploring the myriad of capabilities that SAP can offer.

You will need an SAP S-user (me.sap.com) and an SAP account (sap.com) to access the content.

  • SAP Best Practices based on SAP Signavio Process Navigator, alternatively, SAP Signavio Process Explorer is the gateway to get insights into SAP’s process portfolio and leverage related process implementation guidance and reference business and solution architectures. These include business-capability and solution maps, process models, metrics, industry best practices, and SAP best-practice and product recommendations.

 

 

  • SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to explore, plan and implement SAP Fiori, the latest user experience from SAP including all relevant content for the SAP Fiori launchpad.

 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform to explore and understand different architecture patterns which can be implemented using SAP BTP services.

 

  • SAP BTP Guidance Framework for architects, developers, and administrators.

 

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (or Intelligent Enterprise Reference Solution Architecture) to help discover, consume, and create integrations, extensions, and innovations.

 

  • SAP Activate provides a clear guided methodology to deploy, adopt and extend new capabilities with different approaches, e.g., agile or waterfall, cloud, hybrid or on-premise, implementation or upgrade.

 

  • SAP Road Maps support the journey to SAP’s future product portfolio. Before customising, check for future enhancements.

 

  • SAP Customer Influence offers a range of programs that give customers the opportunity to influence SAP software development decisions, request specific enhancements or features, and adopt new innovations early on. Before customising, check (and vote) for opportunities and ideas to speed up SAP’s development and delivery of new requirements. Customers should engage the SAP account team to work internally with the development and product management teams.

 

  • SAP Secure Operations Map ensures smooth operation of SAP solutions by taking action proactively, before severe security problems occur.

 

 

Depending on use case, when the company is ready for transformational initiatives, use the tools and services available to customers:

  • SAP Value Lifecycle Manager for a value analysis, total costs of ownership calculation and more.

 

  • SAP Readiness Check conducts a detailed analysis of our existing SAP system to help plan our digital transformation. For example, SAP S/4HANA Conversion, SAP S/4HANA Upgrade, SAP BW/4HANA Conversion, SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling, SAP Customer Experience Solutions, SAP SuccessFactors Solutions, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge.

 

  • SAP Custom Code Analytics uses aggregated Custom Code Management (CCM) data from your SAP Solution Manager. The application gives an overview about the custom code footprint of the ABAP systems and provides insights about code quality and usage.

 

  • SAP Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services are included in the SAP Enterprise Support contract. After each service, you receive a service report with an executive summary, findings and a detailed action plan to mitigate risks or improve your situation.

 

  • SAP Customer Evolution Kit for cost-free engagement to accelerate digital transformation to the cloud.

 

  • SAP Signavio, Process Insights, discovery edition, the successor of Process Discovery and Pathfinder on Spotlight, can get innovation recommendations based on the usage of the current system.

 

  • SAP AppHaus and its network partners offer different engagement types. From discovering innovation opportunities like rethinking business processes and user interfaces to delivering scalable solutions.

 

  • SAP Cloud Appliance Library offers a quick and easy way to create SAP workloads in our cloud infrastructure.

 

  • SAP Digital Discovery and Assessment is used to plan the scope of a planned SAP S/4HANA project (for SAP partners only).
  • SAP Simplification Item Catalogue helps better prepare for the transition to SAP S/4HANA and SAP BW/4HANA. It provides a description of all relevant changes that might have an impact when converting from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or SAP BW to SAP BW/4HANA.

 

  • SAP Extensibility Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud covers a broad spectrum of topics that allow us to adapt standard business software to our business needs by leveraging the extensibility patterns and sample scenarios from SAP.

 

 

When customising is required (Clean Core), refer to the following frameworks and additional resources:

  • SAP Store is a digital marketplace with over 2000+ extensions where we can discover, try, and buy solutions from SAP partners.

 

  • SAP Partner Finder to find the best SAP partners to meet our needs.

 

  • SAP Cloud Application Programming Model helps build cloud-native applications with maximised productivity at minimised costs, based on proven best practices served out of the box.

 

  • SAP UI Development Toolkit for HTML5 is a platform for developing rich user interfaces for modern web business applications.

 

  • SAP Learning offers free online courses, tutorials, trials, and official SAP help documentation for anyone who wants to build in-demand SAP skills and prepare for SAP certifications.

 

  • SAP Community offers access to experts, knowledge sharing, the latest news, resources, and insights on SAP solutions and technologies.

 

  • SAP for Me is the digital companion to interact with SAP and aggregates important alerts, metrics, and insights about the SAP product portfolio with a single access point.

 

  • Microsoft Azure Architecture Centre on guidance for architecting solutions on Azure using established patterns and practices, including SAP on Azure (as Microsoft is our strategic partner).

 

I hope it also helps guide and drive your project. It is a starting point to prepare your colleagues to support business and tech decisions.

Surely I missed many helpful links here, so feel free to add yours in the comments too. Thanks!

Originally posted on LinkedIn.

entry.title: How To Implement Alert Notification Service in Cloud Application Programming Model (CAPM) Project
entry.content:
Step 1: Go to Subaccount -> Go To Instance and Subscription -> Click Create Button

Step 2: In Service Select Alert Notification and In the Instance Name Section Give Any Name, Leave Other Fields as It Is.

Note: If you did not create any space then you see create space option just below space filed. Just click on that give space name and choose roles and click Create

Step 3: Access SAP Alert Notification Cockpit and Create a Subscription

  1. After creating an instance, it will be listed under Instance and Subscription.

  2. Click on the instance name from the list.

  3. This will open the SAP Alert Notification Cockpit in a new page.

  4. In the cockpit, navigate to the Subscription section.

  5. Click the Create button to start creating a new subscription.

Step 4: Create Subscription with Condition and Action

  1. Create Subscription:

    • Go to the subscription creation section.

    • Enter a random name for the subscription.

    • Click Create to proceed to the next step.

  2. Create Condition:

    • Enter a random name for the condition.

    • In the Condition section, choose the criteria for triggering the email.

      • Example: Set eventType to Contains → Warning.

    • Click Create.

    • If multiple conditions are created, you can select the relevant one in the next step.

    • Click Save and Next to proceed.

  3. Create Action:

    • Select Email as the action type.

    • Click Next.

    • Provide:

      • Action Name

      • Email Address of the recipient.

      • Subject Template (for email header)

      • Payload Template (for email body)

    • Click Create.

    • On the next page, select the appropriate action and click Save.

Notes:

  • Note 1: You can create Actions and Conditions separately by navigating directly to the Action or Condition tabs from the left-side menu.

  • Note 2: The recipient email address will receive a confirmation email. They must accept it to start receiving notifications.

  • Note 3: To send Email Subject and Body dynamically from your application payload, leave the Subject Template and Payload Template fields blank.

Step 5: Create a Service Key for the Alert Notification Instance

  1. After setting up the Alert Notification instance, locate it in the list.

  2. Click on the arrow ( > ) next to the instance name.

  3. A panel will open on the right side of the screen.

  4. In the right panel, look for the Service Key section.

  5. Click the Create button.

  6. A popup window will appear for creating a new service key.

  7. Enter a name for the service key.

  8. Click Create to generate the key.

 

 

Step 6: Create Destination for Use in CAPM Application

  1. After creating the service key, the next step is to create a destination.

  2. This destination will be used in your CAPM application.

  3. To get the required details (URL, Client ID, Client Secret):

    • Click on the instance.

    • Select View Credentials to open the service key details.

    • Copy the URL, Client ID, and Client Secret from the credentials.

  4. In the left-side panel, go to Connectivity → Destinations.

  5. Click the Create Destination button.

  6. Fill in the destination details as shown in the reference screenshot:

    • URL

    • Authentication method

    • Client ID

    • Client Secret

    • Any other required fields

  7. Click Create to save the destination.

 

Step 7: Integrate Alert Notification in a CAPM Application

  1. Open or create a CAPM application where you want to integrate the alert notification.

  2. In the CAPM application, set up a connection to the destination you created earlier.

  3. Use the standard API endpoint to trigger the notification:

     
    /cf/producer/v1/resource-events

  4. This endpoint allows your application to send events to the SAP Alert Notification service.

  5. For full API details and supported parameters, refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 
 
 

Step 8: Test Using Postman

  1. Deploy your service.

  2. Use the deployed service URL.

  3. Get the Client ID and Client Secret from the environment variable under the xsuaa section and use it in postman for authentication using oAuth 2.0.

 
 

Conclusion:

By following the above steps, we have successfully set up and integrated SAP Alert Notification with a CAPM application. Starting from creating the alert notification instance, setting up conditions and actions, generating a service key, creating a destination, and finally integrating and testing the service using Postman — the entire process ensures that your application can send real-time alerts based on defined events. This integration enhances monitoring and improves responsiveness to critical events in your system.
